title: Unctuous Thai Pork Curry
description: A rich and flavorful Thai curry made with fermented ingredients and maple syrup, perfect for a comforting meal. This dish is braised in a claypot and is friendly to Buddhist temple dietary restrictions.
instructions: Heat the oil in a claypot over medium heat.
Add the curry paste and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the pork and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the maple syrup, fish sauce, and fermented soybeans, and stir to combine.
Pour in the coconut milk and add the kaffir lime leaves.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 2 hours.
Stir in the chopped Thai basil and cook for an additional 10 minutes.
Serve the curry hot, garnished with additional basil if desired.
